title: excoecaria biglandulosa var. aubletianum
intro: Excoecaria biglandulosa var. aubletianum, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant contains toxic compounds called phorbol esters, which have been found to have medicinal properties in traditional medicine, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Excoecaria biglandulosa var. aubletianum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make topical creams and ointments, as well as tinctures and teas, but its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
